until now, when your agent sent a voice note, it had to improvise t…

excited to have built Voice Personas for @openclaw!

until now, when your agent sent a voice note, it had to improvise the “flair” every time, which rarely worked

with Personas, your agent’s SOUL can get passed deterministically into every single voice memo, so they SOUND like you always imagined

Install/update got harder to wedge: staged plugin runtime deps, disk-space warnings, package validation, and cleaner repair paths.

If update still gets weird:
curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash
Browser control got safer tab IDs, URL-enriched snapshots, headless one-shot starts, CDP readiness tuning, and better doctor probes. https://docs.openclaw.ai/browser

OpenClaw 2026.4.25 🦞

🔊 TTS got serious

📊 OTEL can see the weird stuff
🛠️ Browser + install/update fixes

Less mystery, more machinery.
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.25

Browser automation got more practical: coordinate clicks, profile-level headless overrides, stable tab reuse, stale-lock recovery, and longer default action budgets. https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/browser
Talk and Voice Call can now hand deeper questions to the full OpenClaw agent, so realtime voice stays fast but can still reach tools when it needs to. https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/voice-call

OpenClaw 2026.4.24 🦞

☎️ Voice calls can now reach the full agent

More reach. Less duct tape.
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.24

A place to hang out, share your Clawdis setups, ask questions, and help each other build cool stuff with AI assistants.

Join the crustacean crew: https://discord.gg/qkhbAGHRBT
DeepSeek V4 Flash is now the onboarding default, with V4 Pro in the catalog and replay/thinking fixes for follow-up tool-call turns. https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/deepseek
RT @stevehou: “I used to be very skeptical and resistant towards @openclaw, but after trying it myself for a short period, I think it is an…

Security pass: tighter mobile cleartext rules, safer Teams/Discord/QQBot auth paths, webhook SecretRefs re-resolve on reload, and agent config edits are fail-closed by default. https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security

Channels got polish: Telegram markdown images become media replies, Slack MPIMs behave like groups, WhatsApp setup avoids eager Baileys loading, and media delivery got less brittle. https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels

Sharper models, smoother claws.
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.23

“I used to be very skeptical and resistant towards @openclaw, but after trying it myself for a short period, I think it is an epoch-defining product. Within 3 days, it went from a product with humanist design to taking over part of my life/work to aiding my research.” - @_LuoFuli

Yes, our latest special guest is Fuli Luo @_LuoFuli .

The second battle in the global large model arms race has begun: shifting from the Chat era dominated by pre-training to the Agent era driven by post-training.

This marks Fuli Luo’s first-ever interview, as well as her first in-depth technical conversation. We talked systematically about the massive AI upheaval triggered by technological breakthroughs including Claude Opus 4.6 and OpenClaw in 2026, along with its subsequent structural impacts across the industry.

Amid the fierce large-model arms race, the world around us is undergoing brutally rapid changes—even for researchers who train models firsthand.

“I used to believe our work was highly creative, and could never be simplified into fixed skills or standardized workflows. But now I realize it can be automated after all. If that’s possible, can models train stronger models on their own? Can they achieve iterative improvement through self-evolution? This is exactly what will unfold in the next couple of years,” Fuli Luo says.

As human knowledge and wisdom are internalized into model capabilities, what will humanity pursue in the future? Is our society truly ready for this tsunami-scale technological revolution?

All in all, this is an information-dense dialogue. It reveals how an AI lab makes strategic technical bets, allocates resources, and adjusts organizational structure and team planning amid a major paradigm shift. At the core of its response to drastic change lies its established culture and core values.

Though lengthy and technically intensive, we hope this conversation brings great insights to every viewer. 

Our podcast, video episode and article are released simultaneously across platforms, with English subtitles provided to assist non-Chinese-speaking audiences.

Luo Fuli: OpenClaw, Agent Frameworks — The AI Paradigm Has Already Chang... https://youtu.be/V9eI-t3TApE?si=WBSoMmEQe2x9JZOS 来自 @YouTube

`openclaw plugins enable tokenjuice` lets you use tokenjuice to reduce the tokens used for tool calls.

http://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/tokenjuice

Local TUI mode runs terminal chats without a Gateway, while still keeping plugin approval gates in place. Handy for quick local sessions and offline-ish debugging.

https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/tui

Two experiments in the next @openclaw to address some "GPT is lazy" issues: 
1) Strict mode:

agents.defaults.embeddedPi.executionContract = "strict-agentic"

This tells GPT-5.x to keep working: read more code, call tools, make changes, or return a real blocker instead of stopping at “here’s the plan.” https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/openai
